<<case study of chelation therapy and autism treatment>>

a retired lithuanian opera singer is organizing a concert to celebrate lithuanian culture. she plans to include both traditional lithuanian songs and operatic arias. she decides to perform a total of 18 pieces, with twice as many traditional songs as operatic arias. she also wants to feature 3 guest performers, each performing 2 additional pieces. to determine how many traditional lithuanian songs she will perform herself, we can calculate the total number of pieces and then subtract the number of operatic arias. 

the singer has decided to perform 18 pieces in total. if she performs twice as many traditional songs as operatic arias, let x represent the number of operatic arias. then the number of traditional songs would be 2x. the total number of pieces is x + 2x = 3x. setting this equal to 18, we get 3x = 18, so x = 6. this means she will perform 6 operatic arias and 12 traditional songs. 

she also wants to feature 3 guest performers, each performing 2 additional pieces. this adds 6 more pieces to the total. so the total number of pieces she will perform is 18 + 6 = 24. the number of traditional songs she will perform is 12, as calculated earlier.
